UPSC examination allegedly has been quite similar over the years.

you know, the the exam structure, you know, there's a I think preliminary stage, then there's a mains examination and then there's an interview. Because see syllabus though the syllabus you are saying is to some extent is static it was not changed from last let's say 10 years but the way questions are being asked in the

have eventually changed roughly every year they are asking with some different format different pattern. What what different format what key differences have you noticed? See initially if you see the papers before 2016 they were too much to some extent focusing upon the factual things. After that the conceptual related aspects and that's why you will observe that the cut off started getting declined. There was reduction of the cut off as far as the UPSC prelims is concerned. Later on they started once they thought that yes there

will be to some extent human might be thinking in that particular manner and the syllabus considering that the syllabus is limited. Now we have to introduce certain changes. made as a part of prelims examination. So it's not like that they are making every stage difficult. They are trying to ensure that what practical need are there from the perspective of an administrator at the same point of time keeping in mind that we can take the limited aspirants only. But yes, to some extent it is still justified that the B UPSC is conducting the examination. Because every year they are changing. Yeah, one last question, sir. What kind of if any stresses that you feel students face while preparing for UPSC? No, no, I mean your your view, your view of because see what they are going through they they know better. But as an observer and especially as a teacher,

You get to observe them in a in a different way. See again, here it's not like one shoe fit for all. Means aspirant who is coming at 17, aspirant who is coming at 21, aspirant who is coming at 23, 24, maybe after a job. So they faces different different type of stress. The first they are quite relaxed, the 17, 18, 19, they are comparatively relaxed at the same time and there is no much hurry. But at the same point of time, sometimes what they do, they tend to prioritize this preparation over the graduation and

what they tend to do, they tend to neglect completely the graduation, that should not be done. Because at the end of the day if the graduation is not done well, eventually a lot of things which you could utilize as part of your potential in the administration, you might not be able to do. Because you are in the mad race and you are just preparing for this examination. A person who is coming at 21-22, they have energy, at the same point of time they have time available, they can have the resilience also, they can spend one or two year in this preparation. The pressure is there, yes, competitive pressure is there and

keep on increasing. It's there for everyone. But then the pressure is comparatively lesser. But a person who's coming after 24, 25 who have left their job, eventually now coming to a particular preparation, what stress they face is there is a type of existential type of crisis that if they are not able to clear this in one or let's say in the second attempt, then eventually there is a pressure building up from the side of family, from the side of their own mental resilience that they might not be able to continue for a long period of time. And that's what is the thing that this examination

is telling you when to enter. But this particular examination cycle is not telling you when to exit. And this is a practical plan that everyone should be having when to exit from this cycle because otherwise it will be too much cumbersome you will keep on loading yourself and then eventually the stress keeps on piling upon you. And that might create a lot of issues. And see at the end of the day it's this examination. Many students fail to realize that after three, four, five attempts. And see maybe there are case studies where the sixth attempt maybe people

they have cleared, they every year they clear. But the point is that there is something behind the curtain that is working for them. Maybe from side family, maybe from the side of their own educational background. So something they are keeping in their mind. But those who are maybe coming from some background might not be having that strong graduation, might not be able to do well with the things that they are going to pursue after graduation, at the same point of time they are not having any plan B. So in that cases the pressure that is mounting up on

those boarding the respirant is very very high. And that might create a lot of serious issues. So that's why a student should be having that when to exit this preparation. Very good point Vishal. I mean, do you think do you don't believe that a lot of people come with that plan B? It's not necessary that every time like you should be coming initially only with plan B, but at least after a certain point you should be having trying to explore certain things because see, after certain point of time maybe

after a lot of this artificial intelligence going on, maybe the administrative task might also be obsolete. The chances are high that over a period of time the number of vacancies might reduce. So try to seek certain other alternative opportunities also. There are many other opportunities. Any indication as to when to leave the examination cycle because See, it's completely

personal. I cannot suggest that when one should be leaving, but there are certain factors to be kept in mind. Are you having any plan B available? Are you having some backup source of income available? Is your family financially stable and strong? They can keep you for three, four years when you are not earning. Are you in a mental position in order to continue the burden of this preparation? If all the things are yes, then in that particular case, keep on preparing.
